title: SACRAMENTO
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 2602 requires informed consent by performers in the film, music, and other entertainment sectors if studios wish to utilize their digital replicas created through AI technology.
Digital replica is defined as a computer-generated, highly realistic electronic representation that is identifiable as the voice or visual likeness of an individual.
Some contracts in the entertainment industry include clauses that grant full use of a performer’s voice and likeness forever.
Non-union performers and voice actors, particularly in video games, are especially vulnerable to exploitation regarding their digital replicas.
AB 2602 ensures that performers provide informed consent and are represented by a lawyer or union representative before transferring rights to their digital replicas.
AB 2602 strengthens a working performer’s fundamental right to control their voice and likeness and consent to how it’s used.
The legislation is presented as a model for how AI regulation should occur across all industries.
